description.en: A flat, leaf-shaped Provençal loaf slashed into a lattice so it bakes thin and mostly crust, its olive-oil dough studded with black olives and herbes de Provence. Descended from the Roman panis focacius.
description.de: Ein flaches, blattförmiges provenzalisches Brot mit gitterartigen Einschnitten, das dünn und fast nur aus Kruste bäckt; der Olivenölteig ist gespickt mit schwarzen Oliven und Kräutern der Provence. Abstammend vom römischen panis focacius.
description.es: Una hogaza provenzal plana, en forma de hoja, cortada en celosía para que se hornee fina y casi toda corteza; su masa con aceite de oliva va salpicada de aceitunas negras y hierbas de Provenza. Desciende del panis focacius romano.
description.fr: Un pain provençal plat en forme de feuille, entaillé en réseau pour cuire fin et croustillant à souhait, à la pâte à l'huile d'olive parsemée d'olives noires et d'herbes de Provence. Descendant du panis focacius romain.

origin.note: Bakers once fired the first fougasse into the wood oven to test its heat before the real bake of the day, long before it became a dish in its own right.

keeping.note: Best warm the day it's baked; a hot oven for a few minutes revives it the next day.

method.title: Bloom the yeast
method.summary: Dissolve the yeast in the warm water.

method.title: Mix
method.summary: Work in the flour, olive oil and salt; knead to a smooth, supple dough.

method.title: First rise
method.summary: Let rise about an hour until doubled.

method.title: Add olives and herbs
method.summary: Gently fold in the olives and herbes de Provence, then pat out into a flat oval.

method.title: Slash
method.summary: Cut the classic leaf-vein pattern through the dough and pull the slashes open so they bake apart.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Proof briefly, brush with olive oil, and bake hot until golden and crisp.
